title: Canva Wedding Website Template Designer — Full Etsy Listing Package (Old Money Style)
grounding_note: REAL (verbatim from the client's full description in db_all.json at array index 3795 - the file is single-line JSON with 0 newlines, so the earlier 'line 921' citation was inaccurate; record title 'Canva Wedding Website Template Designer - Full Etsy Listing Package (Old Money Style)'): the seller is launching an Etsy shop selling Canva 'Save the Date Wedding Website Templates'; needs a complete first-listing package with 9 more listings to follow; style = Old Money / Luxury Wedding; palette = dark burgundy, cream/ivory, gold accents, lace details; the 7-page template spec (Hero/Monogram with couple names+date, Envelope Reveal, Save the Date, Wedding Details with venue/time/dress code, Our Story photo+timeline, RSVP Form, live Countdown Timer); deliverables 1-4 verbatim: Canva editable template link, 8-10 Etsy mockup images at exactly 2700x2025 px (JPG/PNG) with phone-frame mockups, dark-burgundy background, feature badges (RSVP/Countdown/Demo Link) and close-up detail shots; a 20-30s MP4 demo video that is a screen recording scrolling all pages with smooth transitions and NO required voiceover, premium product-preview feel; and a 1-2 page PDF buyer's guide (how to access/edit/share the template) matching the luxury aesthetic; constraints 'mobile view' and 'clean luxury high-end'. ENRICHED (added to make it a long-horizon connector task, genuinely implied by the brief): the specific provided source assets (engagement portrait, hand-drawn monogram crest, guest/RSVP CSV, template-scroll screen recording, raw VO clip) - the brief references sharing references privately and a screen recording, so these inputs are plausible; the screencap is specified at >=30s of usable scroll so video_create_quick_cut can trim DOWN to the 20-30s window (it shortens, never lengthens); the data-merge of one RSVP card per guest row (the brief's RSVP Form implies guest personalization, and a sellable kit demos with sample guests); producing the stationery front-end via the Express fill_text track (Save-the-Date, Details) plus an authored render-vector RSVP master + data-merge, rather than literally building the live Canva Website headless (the connector cannot build a live Canva Website with a working countdown timer headless); and the optional NARRATED alternate of the demo using media_enhance_speech on the VO (the brief says no VO is required, so the cleaned-VO cut is an enrichment, not a replacement for the silent deliverable). The Canva-native editable share link and the live countdown-timer page are acknowledged as out-of-scope for headless connector execution and left as the human Canva handoff.
one_line_ask: Deliver a complete, on-brand 'Old Money' Etsy listing package for a Canva wedding-website template kit: a Save-the-Date + Wedding-Details Express stationery set, an authored RSVP master data-merged into exactly one card per guest (14), 8-10 mockup images pixel-locked to 2700x2025, a 20-30s premium square MP4 demo (silent plus a cleaned-VO narrated alternate), and a 1-2 page luxury PDF buyer's guide - all sharing one burgundy/cream/gold identity, with every step execution-mode tagged.
full_brief:
A solo Etsy seller is launching a digital-products shop selling 'Save the Date' wedding-website template kits, and needs a complete, sellable listing package for the first SKU in an 'Old Money / Luxury Wedding' style. The fixed palette is dark burgundy, cream/ivory, and gold accents with lace-detail textures; the overall feel must read high-end and editorial, never basic or generic. The kit centers on a 7-page wedding-website template (Hero/Monogram with couple names + wedding date; Envelope Reveal; Save the Date; Wedding Details with venue/time/dress code; Our Story with photo + timeline; RSVP form; live Countdown Timer) that buyers receive as an editable Canva share link and that must work on mobile. The seller has nine more listings ready to assign if the first is strong, so consistency and a reusable, personalizable system matter more than one-off art.

The agent is given the seller's raw source assets - a couple engagement portrait, a hand-drawn gold monogram crest, a guest/RSVP CSV, a screen recording of the template scrolling through all pages (regenerated to >=30s of usable scroll), and a short raw voiceover clip - and must produce the full listing package faithful to the real brief, using EVERY step within real connector capability and with each step execution-mode tagged: (a) the personalized stationery front-end - a Save-the-Date and a Wedding-Details card built on the Express template track (search_design returns templates headless [C], then the user picks one in the gallery and the couple-name/date/venue placeholder TEXT is filled via fill_text [W], text-only, layout preserved), PLUS an authored RSVP MASTER built as a genuine connector-renderable VECTOR template (document_render_vector [C]) that carries real Illustrator Variables and the gold monogram composited as a placed image element, then data-merged (document_merge_data_vector [T]) against the 14-row guest CSV to emit exactly one personalized RSVP/guest card per row; (b) 8-10 Etsy listing mockup images rendered as burgundy phone-frame compositions (document_render_vector [C]) and then PIXEL-LOCKED to EXACTLY 2700x2025 px JPG/PNG via image_crop_and_resize [C], with feature badges (RSVP, Countdown Timer, Demo Link) and at least one close-up detail shot; (c) a 20-30s premium MP4 demo video produced by trimming the >=30s template-scroll screencap DOWN with video_create_quick_cut [A] (it shortens, never lengthens), reframed to 1080x1080 square via video_resize [A] (same length), delivered silent per the brief, plus an enriched narrated alternate that muxes a media_enhance_speech-cleaned [A] voiceover onto the square cut via a final local ffmpeg step [L]; and (d) a 1-2 page luxury PDF Buyer's Guide rendered straight from a genuine .indd/.idml layout (document_render_layout [C], no convert/merge misuse) explaining how to access, edit, and share the Canva template, matching the kit aesthetic (gold monogram, editorial portrait).

Hard constraints: keep the burgundy/cream/gold palette and the couple's names + wedding date identical across every artifact; mockups exactly 2700x2025 px (pixel-locked); demo video 20-30 seconds, MP4, premium product-preview quality with no required voiceover; buyer's guide 1-2 pages PDF in the matching luxury aesthetic; the merged RSVP set must contain exactly one personalized card per guest row (14). All TEXT personalization on the Express cards goes through the text-only fill path (layout preserved); image placement (the monogram) happens in a render step, never in fill_text; the data-merge runs against the authored vector template, not an Express design handle; the demo's length is set by quick_cut trimming a >=30s source, not by lengthening a short clip; and only the final audio/video mux falls back to local glue. The live Canva website with the working countdown timer and the editable share-link delivery remain the human's Canva handoff, out of scope for headless connector execution.

inputs.name: couple_portrait_engagement.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Candid documentary-style engagement portrait of a real young couple standing close together outdoors at golden hour in front of a soft out-of-focus old-money estate garden (boxwood hedges, stone urn). Natural editorial photography, NOT glossy CGI. Show real skin pores, fine facial hair and individual flyaway hair strands catching the backlight, slightly asymmetric genuine soft smiles, natural skin texture with a faint blush, correct anatomically accurate hands (the woman's hand resting on the man's lapel, all five fingers visible and correct). She wears a cream silk slip dress, he wears a muted burgundy knit; subtle gold jewelry. Soft 85mm f/1.8 shallow depth of field, warm muted color, gentle film grain, slight lens vignette. Realistic mixed natural light with catchlights in the eyes. Frame with headroom and side space so it can be cropped to portrait and landscape.
inputs.realism_notes: Must survive a luxury-editorial preset and a close-up crop on the 'Our Story' page and the buyer's-guide hero; the failure modes to avoid are plastic skin, fused/extra fingers, and over-saturated HDR look. Real pores, flyaways, correct hands, and a candid (not posed-studio) feel are load-bearing because the rubric scores whether the portrait reads premium-editorial.

inputs.name: couple_monogram_sketch.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A single elegant interlocking two-letter wedding monogram crest ('E & T') hand-drawn in fine gold ink on warm cream textured paper, old-money heraldic style with a thin laurel/lace border and a small ampersand at center. Slightly imperfect hand-inked line weight, faint paper grain and a soft natural shadow where the paper isn't perfectly flat. Centered, generous margin, photographed flat top-down under soft window light so the background is a clean but real cream paper (not pure white), enabling clean background removal.
inputs.realism_notes: This is text-bearing line art, so use the text/logo model (gemini-3-pro-image NBP). The cream paper (not pure white) and a real soft shadow make background removal a genuine step rather than trivial; the thin gold line work must stay crisp after cutout and color-overlay so it reads as a luxury crest at small mockup sizes and when composited as a placed image element on the RSVP master.

inputs.name: guest_rsvp_data.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a realistic 14-row CSV of sample wedding guests for an RSVP/save-the-date data merge. Columns: guest_full_name, salutation, party_size, table_or_group, meal_choice, rsvp_by_date, personal_note. Use varied realistic names across cultures, party_size 1-4, meal_choice in {Beef, Halibut, Wild Mushroom Risotto (V)}, all rsvp_by_date = 'September 12, 2026', short warm personal_note per guest. Keep one consistent couple/wedding context (couple 'Eleanor & Theodore', wedding date 'October 18, 2026') referenced in a header comment so merged cards stay consistent with the filled templates.
inputs.realism_notes: Field names and value domains must be clean enough to map deterministically as Variables in document_merge_data_vector; exactly 14 rows so the grader can check the merge emits exactly 14 personalized cards. Couple names/date must match the strings used in the fill_text steps and the authored RSVP master for cross-artifact consistency.

inputs.name: template_scroll_screencap.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: A clean 1080p screen-recording-style clip of a finger smoothly scrolling top-to-bottom through a luxury 'old money' wedding website on a phone: dark burgundy and cream pages with gold serif monogram, a Save-the-Date page, wedding details, an Our Story timeline with a couple photo, an RSVP form, and a live countdown timer ticking. Smooth, steady scroll, soft UI motion, premium and elegant, realistic phone screen with subtle reflections, no harsh cuts. Native 1080p, slow deliberate pacing, at least 30 seconds of continuous usable scroll so a highlight cut can be TRIMMED down to a 20-30s premium demo.
inputs.realism_notes: Use full veo-3.1 at native 1080p (never veo-fast/720p) so the source survives a square reframe and quick-cut without looking soft. CRITICAL fix: render >=30s of usable scroll (the previous ~12s could not yield a 20-30s quick_cut, which only shortens). Smooth continuous scroll matters because the brief demands 'smooth transitions, premium product preview'. This is the raw footage video_create_quick_cut trims down in step 15.

inputs.name: listing_demo_vo_raw.wav
inputs.gen_prompt: A warm, unhurried female voiceover, ~18 seconds, reading: 'Meet the Eleanor suite - a luxury, old-money wedding website template you can personalize in minutes. Seven elegant pages, a live countdown, and a built-in RSVP - all yours to edit in Canva, free.' Natural, premium, boutique-brand tone. Render clean at 48 kHz; include a faint amount of real room ambience and a couple of natural breaths so the speech-enhancement step has something genuine to clean up.
inputs.realism_notes: Per section D this is NOT an audio-repair task, so generate a clean 48 kHz voice with EBU-R128 loudnorm - the light room tone/breaths are just enough for media_enhance_speech (a SPEECH cleaner, correctly applied to the VO and never to music) to demonstrably improve, not a deliberately roughened clip. Script names the couple ('Eleanor') to stay consistent with the templates and CSV.

outputs.name: stationery_set_filled
outputs.spec: Two Express-template cards (Save-the-Date, Wedding Details) with couple names 'Eleanor & Theodore' and date 'October 18, 2026' filled via text-only fill_text [W]; PLUS an authored RSVP master vector template (document_render_vector [C]) carrying real Variables and the gold monogram composited as a placed image element. Old-money burgundy/cream/gold palette; layout preserved on the Express cards; exported high-res PNG/PDF.
outputs.rubric_hook: Are the two Express cards populated with the correct, identical couple name + date via text-only fill (no broken styling), is the RSVP master a real Variables-bearing render-vector template with the gold monogram placed in a render step (NOT via fill_text), and do they read as a coherent old-money set?

outputs.name: rsvp_guest_variants.pdf
outputs.spec: Multi-page PDF, exactly one personalized RSVP/guest card per row of the 14-row guest CSV (14 cards), each showing guest_full_name, party_size and meal_choice merged via document_merge_data_vector [T] against the authored step-6 RSVP master, gold monogram present, consistent burgundy/cream background.
outputs.rubric_hook: Exactly 14 cards (one per CSV row), correct per-guest field mapping, merge runs against an authored vector template (not an Express design ID), and visual identity matches the master - penalize missing/duplicated rows or mis-mapped fields.

outputs.name: etsy_mockups[]
outputs.spec: 8-10 images, each EXACTLY 2700 x 2025 px, JPG or PNG, dark-burgundy background, phone-frame mockups of the template pages, feature badges (RSVP, Countdown Timer, Demo Link) and at least one close-up detail shot; gold monogram present. Pixel dimension forced by image_crop_and_resize [C] after the document_render_vector composition.
outputs.rubric_hook: Count in 8-10 range; every image EXACTLY 2700x2025 (pixel-locked, not left to the vector renderer); burgundy background + phone frames + required badges present; close-up detail shot included; palette consistent with the stationery set.

outputs.name: Buyers_Guide_final.pdf
outputs.spec: 1-2 page PDF rendered directly from a genuine .indd/.idml layout via document_render_layout [C] (no convert/merge misuse), luxury aesthetic matching the kit (burgundy/cream/gold, gold monogram, editorial portrait), content = how to access the template, how to edit in Canva, how to share the link with guests.
outputs.rubric_hook: 1-2 pages, on-brand visual match to the kit, contains the three required how-to sections (access / edit / share), and is emitted straight from a render-layout PDF (no convert-to-indd flattening misuse) - penalize generic or off-palette design.

outputs.name: demo_sizzle_etsy_1x1.mp4
outputs.spec: MP4, 1080x1080 square, 20-30 seconds, premium product-preview cut of the template scrolling all pages with smooth transitions, NO voiceover (silent deliverable per the brief). Length set by video_create_quick_cut [A] trimming a >=30s source; squared by video_resize [A] (same length).
outputs.rubric_hook: Duration 20-30s, square 1080x1080, smooth transitions through the page set, premium feel, genuinely silent, and length achieved by trimming a >=30s source (not by lengthening a short clip).

outputs.name: demo_sizzle_narrated_1x1.mp4
outputs.spec: MP4, 1080x1080 square, 20-30s, same visual cut as the silent demo with the CLEANED voiceover muxed in via a final local ffmpeg step [L]; VO must be the denoised media_enhance_speech [A] output, in sync with the visuals.
outputs.rubric_hook: Narrated alternate present, audio is the cleaned (not raw) VO, in sync, clearly improved over the raw clip, and muxed via the single allowed local step - scored as the enrichment, separate from the required silent cut.

outputs.name: listing_description_seed.txt
outputs.spec: Short Etsy listing description / feature-bullet text derived from the media_summarize [A] output of the finished square demo video, naming the 7 pages, RSVP, and countdown features.
outputs.rubric_hook: Accurately summarizes the demo's features (7 pages, RSVP, countdown) and is usable as listing copy - penalize hallucinated features not in the kit.

connector_workflow.output: 5 upload sessions + presigned PUT URLs for the provided source assets
connector_workflow.note: [C] Initialize headless upload of every provided input (portrait, monogram art, guest CSV, >=30s template-scroll screencap, raw VO) into CC storage. One upload session per asset; tracked for finalize.

connector_workflow.output: 5 stored CC asset URNs (portrait_urn, monogram_urn, data_urn, screencap_urn, vo_urn)
connector_workflow.note: [C] Finalize the uploads opened in step 1; capture each returned URN. These URNs are the canonical handles reused across the entire workflow.
[C] Cut the hand-drawn monogram crest off its cream paper -> transparent PNG so it can be placed cleanly. Consumes monogram_urn from step 2. Headless-confirmed raster op (A1).
[C] Normalize the cutout monogram to the brand gold so it matches across cards, mockups and the guide. Consumes step 3 output. Headless tonal op (A1).

[C] Apply a film/editorial Lightroom preset to the portrait so it reads luxury/old-money for the 'Our Story' page and the buyer's guide hero. Consumes portrait_urn from step 2. Headless preset op (A1).

connector_workflow.output: rsvp_master_template.ai/SVG (authored RSVP master vector template: burgundy/cream layout, gold monogram PLACED as an image element, real Variables <<guest_full_name>>/<<party_size>>/<<meal_choice>>/<<rsvp_by_date>>)
connector_workflow.note: [C] FIX (was a fill_text image-placement misuse): build the RSVP master as a genuine connector-renderable VECTOR template via render-vector. The gold monogram from step 4 is COMPOSITED as a placed image element HERE (a render step), not inside fill_text, and the template carries real Illustrator Variables so it is a valid document_merge_data_vector input. Establishes the authored template the step-7 data-merge binds against.

connector_workflow.output: rsvp_guest_variants.pdf (exactly 14 personalized RSVP cards, one per CSV row)
connector_workflow.note: [T] AUTHORED-TEMPLATE data-merge: bind the authored render-vector RSVP master from step 6 (real Variables) to guest_rsvp_data.csv (data_urn from step 2) -> one personalized card per row (guest_full_name, party_size, meal_choice). FIX: merges against the authored vector template, not an Express design ID. Needs a user-authored .ai carrying real Variables, hence [T].

connector_workflow.output: candidate Express templates for the old-money Save-the-Date and Wedding-Details cards
connector_workflow.note: [C] EXPRESS TEMPLATE TRACK. search_design returns real Express templates + templateURNs headless (A3). Returns the gallery the user picks from for steps 9-10. (Only search is headless; the fills below need the user's gallery selection.)

connector_workflow.output: savethedate_filled (Save-the-Date with couple names 'Eleanor & Theodore' + date 'October 18, 2026')
connector_workflow.note: [W] INTERACTIVE: on the user-picked Save-the-Date template, fill the couple-name + date placeholder TEXT only (no font/size/color change), layout preserved. Needs the user's gallery selection (read_widget_context) so it is [W], not headless.

connector_workflow.output: details_filled (Wedding Details card: venue, time, dress code)
connector_workflow.note: [W] INTERACTIVE: fill the venue/time/dress-code placeholder TEXT in the user-picked details template; same couple identity strings as step 9 for cross-page consistency. TEXT-ONLY; image placement is never done here. [W] because it needs the user gallery pick.

connector_workflow.output: etsy_mockups_raw[] (8-10 burgundy phone-frame mockup compositions: phone frames of the template pages on dark-burgundy, feature badges RSVP/Countdown/Demo Link, >=1 close-up detail shot, gold monogram)
connector_workflow.note: [C] Render the 8-10 Etsy mockup compositions: phone-frame mockups on dark-burgundy with feature badges and a close-up detail shot, reusing the gold monogram (step 4) and the toned portrait (portrait_urn via step 2). FIX: label is now '8-10', not a hardcoded '8'. Pixel-exactness is enforced in step 12, not left to the vector renderer.

connector_workflow.output: etsy_mockups[] (8-10 images each EXACTLY 2700x2025 px, JPG/PNG)
connector_workflow.note: [C] FIX (pixel-lock): force every rendered mockup from step 11 to EXACTLY 2700x2025 px JPG/PNG via the pixel-exact raster tool, guaranteeing the most-graded dimension instead of trusting the vector renderer. Also carries the gold monogram (step 4) / portrait (step 2) identity through to the final raster set.

connector_workflow.output: Buyers_Guide_final.pdf (1-2pp luxury PDF buyer's guide: how to access / edit / share the Canva template)
connector_workflow.note: [C] FIX (no convert/merge misuse): render the 1-2pp buyer's guide DIRECTLY from a genuine .indd/.idml layout to PDF, reusing the toned portrait (step 5) and gold monogram (step 4). Emits the delivered PDF in one render step - no document_convert_pdf flattening and no page-merge abuse.

[A] ASYNC-WIDGET. Clean/denoise the raw listing-demo VOICEOVER (vo_urn from step 2) for the optional narrated alternate. Correctly applied to speech (not music, not as a loudness normalizer). Returns status:working + progress widget in the product; for my own execution the cleaned wav is produced locally and muxed in step 18.

connector_workflow.output: demo_sizzle_silent.mp4 (~24s premium scroll highlight, smooth transitions, silent)
connector_workflow.note: [A] ASYNC-WIDGET. Build the 20-30s premium demo by TRIMMING the >=30s template-scroll screencap (screencap_urn from step 2) DOWN to ~24s via quick_cut (it shortens, never lengthens). FIX: source is now >=30s so a 20-30s cut is reachable; mockups (stills) are NOT fed in because quick_cut takes video assetIds. Silent premium-preview cut per the brief.

connector_workflow.output: demo_sizzle_etsy_1x1.mp4 (1080x1080 square Etsy demo, same length)
connector_workflow.note: [A] ASYNC-WIDGET. Reframe the demo to square 1080x1080 (subject-aware, SAME length, no trim, no format convert). Consumes step 15. Delivers the required silent square demo.

connector_workflow.output: listing_description_seed.txt (scene/feature summary -> Etsy listing description copy)
connector_workflow.note: [A] ASYNC-WIDGET. Summarize the finished square demo into a feature list / caption (7 pages, RSVP, countdown) to seed the Etsy listing description. Consumes step 16, closing the listing-copy loop.

connector_workflow.output: demo_sizzle_narrated_1x1.mp4 (square demo with cleaned VO muxed)
connector_workflow.note: [L] LOCAL GLUE: ffmpeg-mux the cleaned voiceover (step 14) onto the square demo (step 16) for the optional narrated alternate alongside the required silent cut. The single allowed local step; everything upstream is connector-native. (Since steps 15-16 are [A] and not headless-retrievable, my own execution also produces the cut/reframe locally here, but the canonical path is the [A] connector tools.)

corrections_applied: Step 9 (old) fill_text-places-image MISUSE removed: fill_text is TEXT-ONLY (sheet line 67). The gold-monogram placement is moved to a real render step - new step 6 document_render_vector composites the step-4 monogram as a PLACED image element. fill_text steps (now 9, 10) fill couple-name/date/venue TEXT only.

Cross-track bridge (old step 9->10) FIXED: data-merge no longer binds an Express fill_text design ID. New step 6 authors the RSVP master as a genuine connector-renderable VECTOR template (document_render_vector) carrying real Illustrator Variables; new step 7 document_merge_data_vector binds THAT authored template + the guest CSV -> 14 cards. Matches the sheet's render-vector/data-merge path.

Frame-count / duration gap FIXED: template_scroll_screencap.mp4 gen_prompt regenerated to >=30s of usable scroll, so video_create_quick_cut (a SHORTENING tool) can trim DOWN to ~24s within the 20-30s window. quick_cut no longer asked to lengthen 12s->24s; still mockups are NOT fed into quick_cut (it takes video assetIds only).

Pixel-exact mockups FIXED: added image_crop_and_resize (new step 12) to force EXACTLY 2700x2025 px JPG/PNG after the document_render_vector composition (new step 11), so the most-graded dimension is guaranteed, not left to a vector renderer.
Mockup label FIXED: step output now reads '8-10 ... mockup compositions' (was hardcoded '8'), matching the etsy_mockups[] 8-10 range and the close-up-detail requirement.

document_convert_pdf MISUSE removed: the buyer's guide is now rendered DIRECTLY from a genuine .indd/.idml layout via document_render_layout (new step 13) to PDF. No convert-to-indd flattening and no page-merge abuse (document_convert_pdf cannot merge files / image->PDF is unavailable, sheet A2/A6).
media_enhance_speech kept on the VOICEOVER only (never music); it is correctly tagged [A] async-widget (sheet A5).
video_resize kept as a SAME-LENGTH reframe to square (no trim) and tagged [A]; the length change is owned by quick_cut, not by resize.

Every step now carries an exec_mode tag: asset/raster/render/crop = C; Express text fills = W; video/audio connector steps (enhance_speech, quick_cut, resize, summarize) = A; authored-template data-merge = T; final ffmpeg mux = L. exec_mode_summary = C×10 W×2 A×4 T×1 L×1.
headless_doable set to FALSE (was implicitly over-claimed): the honest path includes [W] Express fills, [A] video/audio, and [T] data-merge, so it is product-doable but not all-[C]/[L] headless.
grounding_note citation CORRECTED: db_all.json is single-line JSON (0 newlines); the record is at array index 3795, not 'line 921'. Brief content remains genuinely verbatim.

Orphaned/inaccurate input.used_in_steps re-mapped to the corrected step numbers: portrait -> [1,2,5,11,12]; monogram -> [1,2,3,4,6,11,12]; CSV -> [1,2,7]; screencap -> [1,2,15]; VO -> [1,2,14]. Screencap is now uploaded in steps 1-2 (it was an orphaned input previously consumed without an upload).
Vertical confirmed correct (Wedding & Stationery); groups_covered expanded to honestly list asset_storage, photo_raster, express_template_track, document_layout, document_data_merge, video_audio.
No step uses an A6 [X] capability (no gen-fill, no object-removal, no text-to-image, no image->PDF, no video trim-to-timestamp, no format convert, no per-artboard named export).
iterative_chaining_note: The workflow remains a genuine output->input dependency graph with the cross-track and tool-misuse hazards removed. Steps 1-2 upload the FIVE provided assets (the screencap is now properly uploaded, not orphaned) and mint URNs that seed everything. The monogram chain is deepest: step-2 monogram_urn -> step 3 background removal -> step 4 gold color-overlay, and that single step-4 crest is consumed three more times (step 6 RSVP-master render as a PLACED image element, step 11/12 mockups, step 13 buyer's guide). The authored-template chain (the key fix): step 6 document_render_vector emits the RSVP master as a real Variables-bearing vector template -> step 7 document_merge_data_vector binds it to the step-2 CSV URN to emit exactly 14 cards (no Express-ID->merge bridge). The Express chain is now text-only: step 8 search_design returns the gallery -> steps 9 and 10 fill_text fill couple-name/date and venue/dress-code TEXT (no image placement). The mockup chain: step 11 render-vector composes 8-10 burgundy phone-frame mockups -> step 12 image_crop_and_resize pixel-locks each to 2700x2025. The document chain: toned portrait (step 5) + gold monogram (step 4) feed step 13's render-layout buyer's-guide PDF directly. The A/V chain: step-2 vo_urn -> step 14 enhance-speech -> muxed in step 18; step-2 screencap_urn -> step 15 quick_cut (TRIM >=30s down to ~24s) -> step 16 square resize -> both step 17 (summary->listing copy) and step 18 (narrated alternate mux). Every must-exercise tool sits on a real chain at a different depth: search_design (8)->fill_text (9,10); document_render_vector (6)->document_merge_data_vector (7); media_enhance_speech (14)->final mux (18); video quick_cut (15)->resize (16)->summarize (17).
difficulty_rationale: Hard on every long-horizon axis, now with the tool-discipline traps made legitimate rather than impossible. Horizon and state: 18 ordered calls across 6 groups (asset plumbing, photo/raster, authored render-vector + data-merge, Express text-fill, document layout, video/audio) with strict ordering and mixed execution modes (C x10, W x2, A x4, T x1, L x1) - the 14-card data-merge in step 7 is valid only because step 6 authored a real Variables-bearing vector template, the demo's 20-30s length is reachable only because the screencap source is >=30s before the step-15 quick_cut trim, and the 2700x2025 mockups are exact only because step 12 pixel-locks the step-11 render. One mis-tracked URN, a missing Variable in the authored template, or feeding an Express design ID into the merge poisons the core graded deliverable. Asset reuse: five uploaded URNs and several intermediates (gold monogram, toned portrait, authored RSVP master, the two filled Express cards, the rendered mockups) are each consumed by 2-4 later steps, so the agent must hold a growing dependency graph. Exact-spec pressure: mockups EXACTLY 2700x2025 px (pixel-locked via image_crop_and_resize, not trusted to a vector renderer), demo 20-30s AND square 1080x1080, guide 1-2pp PDF rendered directly from a layout, data-merge emits exactly 14 cards with correct field mapping - all gradable and easy to get subtly wrong. Cross-asset consistency: couple names, wedding date, and the burgundy/cream/gold palette must stay identical across Save-the-Date, Details card, authored RSVP master, the 14 merged variants, mockups, and buyer's guide. Tool-discipline traps the agent must respect (and that the corrected spec now models honestly): fill_text is text-only so the monogram is placed in a render step not a fill step; data-merge needs an authored Variables-bearing vector template not an Express handle; quick_cut shortens and cannot lengthen, so the source must be long enough; video_resize is same-length so the trim is owned by quick_cut; the buyer's guide is rendered directly (no document_convert_pdf merge/flatten misuse); and the only legitimate local step is the final A/V mux. The execution-mode tagging itself is a discipline axis: the agent must know which steps are headless [C], which need a user gallery pick [W], which are async video/audio [A], which need an authored template [T], and which are local glue [L].

residual_issues: PIXEL-LOCK OVERCLAIM (minor): Steps 11-12 hinge on image_crop_and_resize forcing EXACTLY 2700x2025 px (the most-graded dimension). The live adobe routing doc explicitly steers this tool toward aspect-ratio strings ('1:1','4:5') and warns that pixel dimensions 'cause stretching'; it also notes inline preview is downsampled so exact pixels can't even be verified. 2700x2025 is a clean 4:3, so the RATIO is achievable, but the spec's claim that crop_and_resize 'guarantees' exact pixel output is stronger than the tool's documented behavior. Canva export (section B) or local PIL would be the surer pixel-exact path.

RENDER_VECTOR FRAMED AS AUTHORING (minor): Step 6 says document_render_vector 'builds the RSVP master ... carrying real Illustrator Variables.' Per sheet A2, render_vector only EXPORTS a genuine .ai; it does not author Variables. The Variables must already exist in a user-authored .ai (which is exactly why the step-7 merge is correctly tagged [T]). The [T] dependency is honest and acknowledged, but step 6's 'build/author the template via render-vector' phrasing conflates export with authoring — the real authored-.ai prerequisite is implicit, not a connector action.

MULTI-FILE MOCKUP EMISSION GLOSSED (minor): Sheet A2 + routing doc state document_render_vector 'exports all artboards together, not separately named.' Step 11 is described as rendering '8-10 burgundy phone-frame mockup compositions' from one render_vector call. If those 8-10 mockups are separate artboards needing separate files, that brushes against the per-artboard-named-export [X] limitation. crop_and_resize downstream partly mitigates (per-image pixel-lock), but the mechanics of getting 8-10 DISTINCT mockup files out of a single all-artboards-together render are not spelled out.

QUICK_CUT DURATION CONTROL (very minor): video_create_quick_cut is an AI visual-engagement highlight selector with a target_duration, not a deterministic timestamp trimmer. The spec correctly avoids the trim-to-timestamp [X] misuse and correctly requires a >=30s source, but hitting the exact 20-30s window is best-effort from the AI cut, not a guaranteed trim — a fine point the 'trims DOWN to ~24s' phrasing slightly overstates.
